creator: jc@irbs.com
text:
When the arp entry for the Cisco expires, arp requests are issued
for the destination IP rather than the next hop IP which is th
Cisco.

The interface address is 209.36.62.211/29, the next hop Cisco is
209.36.62.210.

12:50:30.006992 arp who-has 198.108.225.232 tell 209.36.62.211
12:50:31.018596 arp who-has 212.210.246.39 tell 209.36.62.211
12:50:32.011115 arp who-has 193.227.69.106 tell 209.36.62.211
12:50:33.008656 arp who-has 193.4.198.10 tell 209.36.62.211
12:50:34.012108 arp who-has 194.51.131.253 tell 209.36.62.211
12:50:55.012083 arp who-has 209.36.62.209 tell 209.36.62.211
12:50:55.014307 arp reply 209.36.62.209 is-at 0:0:c:30:35:45

Also, the arp request for the next hop router manages to be delayed
for host_down_time for some reason.  The machine stops routing for
20 seconds.

Fix: Not sure if this is the correct way to do this but it does solve
the problem.

How-To-Repeat: 
Fast forward packets at a good rate, several hundred per second
probably.  Wait for the next hop router arp entry to expire or
delete it from the arp table.
